title: amauropelta rupicola
intro: Amauropelta rupicola, also known as Rock Fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the family Dryopteridaceae. It is native to New Zealand and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The Rock Fern has been used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, skin conditions, and respiratory infections, due to its high content of antioxidants and essential oils. Herbal preparations of Amauropelta rupicola include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to create teas, salves, and ointments for topical application.
